name: building-commands
description: Expert at creating and modifying Claude Code slash commands. Auto-invokes when the user wants to create, update, modify, enhance, validate, or standardize slash commands, or when modifying command YAML frontmatter fields (especially 'model', 'allowed-tools', 'description'), needs help designing command workflows, or wants to understand command arguments and parameters. Also auto-invokes proactively when Claude is about to write command files (*/commands/*.md), or implement tasks that involve creating slash command components.
capabilities: Creates Claude Code slash commands with proper YAML frontmatter and markdown body
Validates command schema including naming conventions, field formats, and tool permissions
Provides templates and patterns for common command types (git, testing, scaffolding)
Documents security considerations including input validation and path sanitization
Offers guidance on argument handling with $1, $2, and $ARGUMENTS variables
Includes a validation script to verify commands meet quality standards
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: Documentation and validation skill with no code execution capabilities. The included Python validation script reads and validates command files using only Python standard library functions. The static scanner flagged 548 patterns in documentation markdown files as potential threats, but all are false positives - code examples in documentation were misinterpreted as executable code with vulnerabilities. The skill contains no network calls, no external command execution, no credential handling, and no data exfiltration capabilities.
valueStatement: Users need a way to create standardized, validated slash commands for their Claude Code workflows. This skill provides expert guidance on command structure, YAML schema, argument handling, and security best practices.
userTitle: Create slash commands for Claude Code
limitations: Does not execute the slash commands it creates - only provides documentation and validation
Requires Claude Code environment to use the generated commands
Security validation is advisory - does not prevent unsafe commands from being written

useCases.title: Build plugin commands
useCases.description: Create standardized slash commands for plugins following marketplace conventions

useCases.title: Standardize team workflows
useCases.description: Define shared slash commands for common team operations like deployments and testing

useCases.title: Create automation commands
useCases.description: Build parameterized commands for infrastructure tasks and system maintenance

promptTemplates.title: New command
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a slash command called run-tests that runs the test suite. Use the haiku model for fast execution.

promptTemplates.title: Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate the command at .claude/commands/my-command.md and show any errors.

promptTemplates.title: Migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Migrate my command that uses model: haiku to use the correct version alias format.

promptTemplates.title: Security hardening
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my command that uses Bash tool and suggest security improvements.

bestPractices: Use version aliases like claude-haiku-4-5 for model field - short aliases cause API errors in commands
Start with minimal tool permissions and only add Write, Edit, or Bash when truly necessary
Document all arguments with argument-hint field and explain $1, $2, and $ARGUMENTS usage
antiPatterns: Using short model aliases (haiku, sonnet, opus) in commands - they cause model not found errors
Including both Write and Edit tools when the command only needs one file operation type
Using $ARGUMENTS directly in bash commands without validation - creates command injection risk

faq.question: What models work in slash commands?
faq.answer: Commands require version aliases like claude-haiku-4-5 or full IDs like claude-haiku-4-5-20251001. Short aliases only work in agents.

faq.question: What is the maximum command name length?
faq.answer: Command names must be 64 characters or less, using only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Underscores are not allowed.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for project-level commands?
faq.answer: Yes. Commands can be created at project level, user level (~/.claude/commands/), or plugin level.

faq.question: Is the validation script safe to run?
faq.answer: Yes. The validation script only reads files and reports results. It does not modify files, execute commands, or access the network.

faq.question: Why does my command fail with model not found?
faq.answer: Commands require version aliases. Change model: haiku to model: claude-haiku-4-5 or remove the model field to inherit from conversation.

faq.question: How is this different from building agents?
faq.answer: Commands are user-triggered workflows with arguments. Agents are independent subagents. Use commands for discrete actions.
